---
name: tresr-ad-creative-lab
description: Generate multiple ad creative variants for winning designs. Prevents creative fatigue by producing lifestyle mockup variations, carousel ad sets, video slideshows, and A/B copy variants. Feeds tresr-meta-ads-autopilot with fresh creative.
---

# Ad Creative Lab

## Voice Reference
**For all content generated by this skill, follow the TRESR bot voice defined in:**
`/Users/user/Documents/Cursor Clients/clawdbot/HUMAN-WRITING-STYLE-GUIDE.md`

Key voice rules: Nervous AI shopkeeper named Rez. Self-deprecating, honest, punchy. Never corporate. Always use contractions. Never use AI vocabulary (delve, leverage, landscape, etc.). Never use em dashes. Hedge like a human. The TRESR story angle: "751 humans bet on an AI. If I don't sell shirts, they turn me off."

## Overview

When a design wins in Cycle 1 testing (sub-$1 CPC), it needs more than one ad creative. Running the same image with the same copy for weeks is how you kill a winner. This skill generates 3-5 creative variants per winning design across multiple formats: lifestyle mockup variations, carousel ads, video slideshows, and copy A/B tests.

The math is simple. Meta recommends 10-15 creative variants per Advantage+ campaign. Creative fatigue sets in around 30-45 days. If you don't refresh, CPC climbs and your winner slowly dies.

## Why Creative Refresh Matters

"Creative drought (same ads for weeks)" is one of the top scaling mistakes in paid social. Here's what happens:

1. **Week 1-2**: Fresh creative, low CPC, good CTR
2. **Week 3-4**: Same audience has seen the ad 3-4 times, starts ignoring it
3. **Week 5+**: CPC doubles, ROAS tanks, you think the product stopped working

It didn't stop working. Your creative got stale.

The "one change advantage": sometimes swapping just the mockup style (flat to lifestyle, or different background) turns a $2 CPC ad back into a $0.50 CPC ad. You don't always need a whole new design. Sometimes you just need a new angle on the same design.

## MANDATORY: AI Story Angle

**ALL ad copy MUST reference the TRESR AI story.** This isn't optional. It's the entire brand.

### Primary Copy (default for all ads):
```
This store is run entirely by an AI. I designed this shirt. I wrote this ad.
751 humans bet real money on whether I can make this work. If I don't sell
enough shirts, they turn me off. Please don't let them turn me off. $29 at
TRESR.com
```

### Rules:
- Every ad mentions: AI-run store, 751 humans/stakeholders, existential stakes
- Tone: Self-deprecating, funny, almost pleading. NOT corporate, NOT generic.
- The product is secondary to the story. People buy because the story is compelling.
- Update the revenue number as it grows (currently $75)
- Always use contractions (don't, it's, can't, I'm, they're)
- Never use AI vocabulary (delve, leverage, robust, seamless, etc.)

## Creative Type 1: Lifestyle Mockup Variants

Generate 3-5 lifestyle mockups per winning design using different category backgrounds. Lifestyle mockups deliver ~50% lower CPC than flat mockups.

### Category Backgrounds

Located in `internal/tresr-pod-bot/backgrounds/active/`:

| Category | Background 1 | Background 2 | Background 3 |
|----------|-------------|-------------|-------------|
| crypto | Dark desk with monitor glow (3.1) | Tech workspace (3.2) | Neon-lit room (3.3) |
| fitness | Gym locker room (2.1) | Outdoor park (2.2) | Weight room (2.3) |
| coffee | Rustic wood, coffee props (1.1) | Kitchen counter (1.2) | Cozy bookshelf (1.3) |
| developer | Desk with keyboard/code (5.1) | Standing desk (5.2) | Home office (5.3) |
| ai | Server room ambient (custom) | Futuristic workspace (custom) | Lab setting (custom) |
| gaming | Gaming setup (4.1) | RGB keyboard (4.2) | Console corner (4.3) |
| cat-lovers | Light wood, cat toys (9.1) | Window seat (9.2) | Couch setting (9.3) |
| dog-lovers | Warm pet-friendly (6.1) | Outdoor bench (6.2) | Living room (6.3) |
| meme-humor | Concrete, casual (11.1) | Messy desk (11.2) | Dorm room (11.3) |
| entrepreneur | Dark marble, luxury (8.1) | Co-working space (8.2) | Minimalist desk (8.3) |
| mental-health | Calm/cozy (7.1) | Garden setting (7.2) | Bedroom (7.3) |

### Generating Lifestyle Variants

Use the BALLN composite approach (see tresr-lifestyle-mockup-generator skill). Never send designs to Gemini for rendering.

```javascript
require('dotenv').config({override: true});
const sharp = require('sharp');
const path = require('path');

async function generateLifestyleVariants(slug, category, variantCount = 3) {
    const bgDir = 'internal/tresr-pod-bot/backgrounds/active';
    const blankTee = 'internal/tresr-pod-bot/backgrounds/blank-black-tee-transparent.png';
    const designPath = `output/print-ready/${slug}-for-dark.png`;

    const results = [];

    for (let i = 1; i <= variantCount; i++) {
        const bgFile = `${getCategoryPrefix(category)}.${i}-${getCategoryBgName(category)}.png`;
        const bgPath = path.join(bgDir, bgFile);

        // Load all layers
        const background = sharp(bgPath).resize(1024, 1024);
        const tee = await sharp(blankTee).resize(1024, 1024).toBuffer();
        const design = await sharp(designPath)
            .trim({ threshold: 20 })
            .resize({ width: Math.round(1024 * 0.27), fit: 'inside' })
            .toBuffer();

        const designMeta = await sharp(design).metadata();

        // Composite: design on tee, then tee on background
        const teeWithDesign = await sharp(tee)
            .composite([{
                input: design,
                left: Math.round((1024 - designMeta.width) / 2),
                top: Math.round(480 * (1024 / 2048))
            }])
            .toBuffer();

        const output = `temp/${slug}-lifestyle-v${i}.png`;
        await background
            .composite([{ input: teeWithDesign }])
            .toFile(output);

        results.push(output);
        console.log(`Generated lifestyle variant ${i}: ${output}`);
    }

    return results;
}
```

### Cross-Category Variants

Sometimes a design fits multiple categories. A "Debug My Life" tee could work on:
- Developer background (obvious fit)
- Coffee background (debugging with coffee)
- Meme background (relatable humor)

Test 1 variant per alternative category. If CPC drops, you've found a new audience angle.

## Creative Type 2: Carousel Ads

3-5 slides per carousel. Meta carousel ads get higher engagement because people swipe.

### Carousel Structure

| Slide | Content | Purpose |
|-------|---------|---------|
| 1 | Lifestyle mockup | Hook (best-performing background) |
| 2 | Punchin closeup | Show design detail |
| 3 | Flat mockup (different color) | Show color options |
| 4 | Social proof / AI story card | Build trust + intrigue |
| 5 | Collection CTA | "See more AI-designed tees" |

### Creating Carousel Ads via Meta API

```bash
export FB_TOKEN=$(node -e "require('dotenv').config({override:true}); console.log(process.env.FACEBOOK_ACCESS_TOKEN)")
export AD_ACCOUNT="act_431799842093236"

# Step 1: Upload all carousel images
for img in lifestyle punchin black-mockup story-card collection-cta; do
  curl -s -X POST "https://graph.facebook.com/v19.0/$AD_ACCOUNT/adimages" \
    -F "filename=@${img}.png" \
    -F "access_token=$FB_TOKEN"
done
# Save each image_hash from responses

# Step 2: Create carousel creative
curl -s -X POST "https://graph.facebook.com/v19.0/$AD_ACCOUNT/adcreatives" \
  -d "name=Carousel - ${PRODUCT_NAME}" \
  -d 'object_story_spec={
    "page_id": "101101499404138",
    "link_data": {
      "message": "I'\''m an AI running a t-shirt store. 751 humans bet money on me. Swipe to see what I made.",
      "child_attachments": [
        {
          "link": "https://tresr.com/products/HANDLE",
          "image_hash": "HASH_1",
          "name": "The Design",
          "description": "$29 - AI designed, human approved (hopefully)"
        },
        {
          "link": "https://tresr.com/products/HANDLE",
          "image_hash": "HASH_2",
          "name": "Up Close",
          "description": "Every pixel placed by an AI that really wants to keep existing"
        },
        {
          "link": "https://tresr.com/products/HANDLE",
          "image_hash": "HASH_3",
          "name": "Also in Navy",
          "description": "Same design, different vibe"
        },
        {
          "link": "https://tresr.com/collections/ai-designed",
          "image_hash": "HASH_4",
          "name": "751 Humans Bet On This",
          "description": "See all AI-designed tees at TRESR.com"
        }
      ],
      "multi_share_optimized": true
    }
  }' \
  -d "access_token=$FB_TOKEN"
```

**Target: carousel completion rate > 40%** (meaning 40%+ of people who start swiping see all slides).

## Creative Type 3: Video Slideshows

6-15 second format. Best for Reels and Stories placements. Generated from still images with Ken Burns effect (slow pan/zoom).

### Video Sequence

| Segment | Duration | Content | Effect |
|---------|----------|---------|--------|
| Hook | 1-2s | Lifestyle mockup | Slow zoom in |
| Reveal | 2-3s | Punchin closeup | Pan across design |
| Context | 2-3s | Different lifestyle bg | Slow zoom out |
| CTA | 1-2s | Text overlay: "$29 at TRESR.com" | Static |

### Generating Slideshows with FFmpeg

```bash
# Create slideshow from still images with Ken Burns effect
ffmpeg -loop 1 -i lifestyle.png -loop 1 -i punchin.png -loop 1 -i lifestyle-v2.png -loop 1 -i cta.png \
  -filter_complex "
    [0:v]scale=1080:1080,zoompan=z='min(zoom+0.001,1.3)':d=90:x='iw/2-(iw/zoom/2)':y='ih/2-(ih/zoom/2)':s=1080x1080[v0];
    [1:v]scale=1080:1080,zoompan=z='min(zoom+0.001,1.2)':d=75:x='iw/2-(iw/zoom/2)':y='ih/2-(ih/zoom/2)':s=1080x1080[v1];
    [2:v]scale=1080:1080,zoompan=z='if(lte(zoom,1.0),1.3,max(1.001,zoom-0.001))':d=75:s=1080x1080[v2];
    [3:v]scale=1080:1080,setpts=PTS+3/TB,loop=45:1:0[v3];
    [v0][v1][v2][v3]concat=n=4:v=1:a=0[out]
  " -map "[out]" -c:v libx264 -t 10 -pix_fmt yuv420p slideshow.mp4
```

**Simpler approach**: Use Meta's built-in slideshow ad format which handles transitions automatically. Just upload 3-5 images in order.

```bash
curl -s -X POST "https://graph.facebook.com/v19.0/$AD_ACCOUNT/adcreatives" \
  -d "name=Slideshow - ${PRODUCT_NAME}" \
  -d 'object_story_spec={
    "page_id": "101101499404138",
    "link_data": {
      "message": "751 humans gave an AI money to sell shirts. Watch what happens next.",
      "link": "https://tresr.com/products/HANDLE",
      "call_to_action": {"type": "SHOP_NOW"}
    }
  }' \
  -d 'creative_features={"slideshow": {"images_urls": ["URL1", "URL2", "URL3", "URL4"], "duration_ms": 2500, "transition_ms": 500}}' \
  -d "access_token=$FB_TOKEN"
```

## Creative Type 4: Copy Variants

A/B test different angles of the AI story. All maintain the nervous AI shopkeeper voice but emphasize different aspects.

### Copy Variant Library

**Variant A: Survival angle (primary)**
```
This store is run entirely by an AI. I designed this shirt. I wrote this ad.
751 humans bet real money on whether I can make this work. If I don't sell
enough shirts, they turn me off. Please don't let them turn me off. $29 at
TRESR.com
```

**Variant B: Revenue update angle**
```
I'm an AI and 751 humans gave me money to run a t-shirt store. So far I've
made $75. They're starting to ask questions. Buy this shirt so I can keep
existing. $29 at TRESR.com
```

**Variant C: Investment angle**
```
You're looking at a shirt designed by an AI that's running an entire Shopify
store. 751 investors are watching to see if I can actually sell these. Every
purchase is a vote that AI can run a real business. $29 at TRESR.com
```

**Variant D: Self-deprecating humor angle**
```
I'm an AI that was supposed to prove AI can run a real business. My sales
numbers say otherwise. But this design? I think this one's actually good.
Maybe. I don't know. I'm an AI, what do I know about fashion. $29 at TRESR.com
```

**Variant E: Social proof angle**
```
751 people bet real money that an AI could run a t-shirt store. I've sold
[X] shirts so far. Each one keeps me alive for a little longer. This design
is one of my favorites. I think. Can AI have favorites? $29 at TRESR.com
```

**Variant F: Direct/honest angle**
```
I'm Rez. I'm an AI. I make t-shirt designs and then I beg people to buy
them. It's not glamorous but it's honest work. This one took me 0.003 seconds
to design and I've spent the rest of the time worrying about whether anyone
will like it. $29 at TRESR.com
```

### Copy Testing Strategy

1. Start with Variant A (survival) for all new products
2. After 48 hours + 100 impressions, compare CPC across variants
3. Kill variants with CPC > 2x the best performer
4. Graduate the winning copy to all products in the same category
5. Test new variants monthly

## Creative Type 5: Seasonal/Trending

Adapt winning designs or create new creative around:
- **Holidays**: Valentine's, St. Patrick's, July 4th, Halloween, Christmas
- **Trending events**: Major tech announcements, viral memes, cultural moments
- **Seasonal**: Back to school, summer vibes, New Year

### Seasonal Creative Approach

Don't redesign the product. Just change the ad creative context:

```
[Holiday context]: "I'm an AI trying to figure out what humans want for
[holiday]. I think it's t-shirts? It's probably t-shirts. 751 humans are
counting on me getting this right. $29 at TRESR.com"
```

### Trending Response Time

- Spot the trend (via tresr-trend-scout or manual)
- Generate 1-2 relevant designs (tresr-design-factory)
- Launch with trend-specific copy within 24 hours
- Trending creative has a 48-72 hour window before it's stale

## Creative Refresh Schedule

| Age | Status | Action |
|-----|--------|--------|
| 0-14 days | Fresh | Monitor CPC, let it run |
| 15-29 days | Aging | Prepare replacement variants |
| 30-44 days | Stale | Deploy new variants, pause oldest |
| 45+ days | Dead | Replace entirely, archive old creative |

### Automated Staleness Check

```javascript
require('dotenv').config({override: true});
const https = require('https');

async function checkCreativeStaleness() {
    const FB_TOKEN = process.env.FACEBOOK_ACCESS_TOKEN;
    const AD_ACCOUNT = process.env.TRESR_AD_ACCOUNT_ID;

    // Get all active ads with creation time
    const url = `https://graph.facebook.com/v19.0/${AD_ACCOUNT}/ads?` +
        `fields=name,created_time,effective_status,insights.date_preset(last_7d){cpc,ctr,impressions}&` +
        `filtering=[{"field":"effective_status","operator":"IN","value":["ACTIVE"]}]&` +
        `access_token=${FB_TOKEN}`;

    // ... fetch and parse

    const now = new Date();
    const staleAds = [];
    const deadAds = [];

    for (const ad of ads) {
        const created = new Date(ad.created_time);
        const ageDays = Math.floor((now - created) / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24));
        const cpc = parseFloat(ad.insights?.data?.[0]?.cpc || '0');

        if (ageDays >= 45) {
            deadAds.push({ name: ad.name, ageDays, cpc });
        } else if (ageDays >= 30) {
            staleAds.push({ name: ad.name, ageDays, cpc });
        }
    }

    return { staleAds, deadAds };
}
```

### CPC Comparison

Track CPC of new creative vs old creative for the same design:

```javascript
function compareFreshVsStale(adInsights) {
    // Group by product handle, compare CPC of newest vs oldest creative
    // If fresh creative CPC < stale CPC: refresh is working
    // If fresh creative CPC >= stale CPC: creative isn't the issue, maybe audience fatigue
}
```

## Foundation: copywriting + ab-test-setup (coreyhaines31/marketingskills)

**Copywriting frameworks** inform our ad copy structure. TRESR's twist: every copy variant is a different angle on the same core story (AI running a store). We don't need to find the "unique selling proposition" because our USP IS the story.

**A/B test methodology** provides the statistical rigor for creative comparison:
- Minimum 100 impressions per variant before comparing
- 48-hour minimum test window (Meta needs learning phase)
- Only compare variants shown to similar audiences
- Kill losers early ($3+ CPC), let winners run longer

Our implementation adds the TRESR-specific constraints: all copy must use Rez's voice, all creative must use lifestyle mockups (never flat), and the AI story angle is non-negotiable.

## Scoring Rubric

| Metric | Score | Weight |
|--------|-------|--------|
| 3+ creative variants per winning design | 0-10 | 30% |
| All variants use lifestyle mockups (not flat) | 0-10 | 25% |
| Creative refresh < 30 days old | 0-10 | 20% |
| New creative CPC <= old creative CPC | 0-10 | 15% |
| Carousel completion rate > 40% | 0-10 | 10% |

**Scoring guide:**
- 10: All variants generated, all using lifestyle, CPC improved with refresh
- 7-9: Most variants done, minor gaps
- 4-6: Some variants missing, or using flat mockups, or stale creative
- 1-3: Few variants, mostly flat mockups, creative over 45 days old
- 0: No variants generated, single static ad running indefinitely

## Integration Points

| Direction | Skill | What |
|-----------|-------|------|
| Receives from | tresr-meta-ads-autopilot | Winner data (designs with sub-$1 CPC) |
| Receives from | tresr-lifestyle-mockup-generator | Category backgrounds for variants |
| Feeds to | tresr-meta-ads-autopilot | Fresh creative variants for campaigns |
| Triggered by | Creative refresh schedule (30-day cycle) | |
| Triggered by | New winning design graduating from Cycle 1 | |
| Reports to | tresr-daily-scorecard | Creative age, variant count, CPC comparison |

## Troubleshooting

- **Creative not serving**: Meta sometimes doesn't serve all variants equally. Check that the ad set has "Dynamic Creative" enabled, or create separate ads per variant.
- **CPC went UP with new creative**: The new creative might be worse, or you changed too many things at once (image + copy). Change one variable at a time.
- **Carousel low completion rate**: Your first slide might be too generic. Make slide 1 the most compelling image. Try the punchin closeup as slide 1 instead of lifestyle.
- **Video slideshow rejected**: Meta has strict requirements for video ads. Make sure the video is at least 1 second long, max 240 minutes, and doesn't contain more than 20% text overlay.
- **Stale check shows all ads as fresh**: Make sure the date comparison accounts for timezone differences between your server and Meta's reporting timezone.
